task_completion.description: The agent successfully loaded the LIDC-IDRI-0001 CT scan into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible. The Data module or window title references 'LIDC-IDRI-0001' or a DICOM series. The slice views show chest CT imaging (lungs visible).

task_completion.description: The agent placed a measurement ruler on the nodule in the Axial plane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Red slice view (Axial), a ruler or line annotation is drawn across a lung nodule. The measurement value is visible near the line.

task_completion.description: The agent placed measurement rulers on the nodule in Coronal and Sagittal planes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Green (Coronal) and Yellow (Sagittal) slice views, rulers or line annotations are drawn across the nodule. This confirms multiplanar assessment.

task_completion.description: The agent verified the measurements using the Markups module table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module panel is open in Slicer, showing a list of at least 3 distinct control points or line measurements corresponding to the different planes.

task_completion.description: The agent generated a JSON report containing the calculated metrics (asphericity, discrepancy, shape).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output displays the content of 'nodule_sizing_report.json'. The JSON keys 'asphericity_percent', 'discrepancy_flag', and 'shape_classification' are visible with values.

task_completion.description: The agent saved both the measurements markup file and the final report to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window or terminal 'ls' command shows both 'multiplanar_measurements.mrk.json' and 'nodule_sizing_report.json' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform measurements rather than estimating or scripting without visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Slicer application window, mouse cursor interaction with slice views, or open modules/panels. The agent should not merely use a terminal to write a JSON file without opening the image.

integrity.description: The agent used the specific Markups/Ruler tool provided by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Slicer toolbar shows the Ruler/Line tool selected, or the annotations on screen are clearly Slicer markup objects (lines with endpoints), not drawn by an external image editor.

integrity.description: The values in the final report appear derived from the software interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the measurement on screen shows '12.5 mm', the report should not show a wildly different number like '5.0 mm'. The values should be plausible for a lung nodule (typically 5-30mm range).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient ID: LIDC-IDRI-0001. The preliminary axial measurement was approx 10mm. The task requires assessing if the nodule is larger in other planes. The application is 3D Slicer; Axial view is typically Red, Sagittal is Yellow, Coronal is Green.
